title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The washer has not filled with water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error E10 on Zanussi washing machines indicates the machine did not reach the required water level within the fill timeout period. Zanussi shares the Electrolux EWX error code platform. This is typically a water supply issue.

causes.cause: Water supply tap turned off or not fully open

symptoms: Fill valve buzzes but no water

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the water supply tap is fully open. Turn off and on to confirm flow.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inlet hose and filters
fixSteps.instruction: Check for kinks. Disconnect and clean the mesh filter screens.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: During fill attempt, check for 230V at valve connector. If voltage present but no water, valve is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water, disconnect hose, swap valve.

whenToCallPro: Only if the inlet valve needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E10 water fill error fix

faq.q: Are Zanussi and Electrolux error codes the same?
faq.a: Yes, Zanussi uses the Electrolux error code platform. E10 means the same on both brands.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause E10?
faq.a: Yes, minimum pressure of about 1 bar is needed.

faq.q: How often should I clean inlet filters?
faq.a: Every 6 months, or more often in hard water areas.

title: Fill Timeout on Pre-Wash
shortDescription: The machine did not fill within the time allowed during the pre-wash phase.
fullDescription: Error E11 is similar to E10 but specifically occurs during the pre-wash filling stage. The same water supply issues apply, but E11 can also indicate a specific pre-wash valve fault on dual-valve models.

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure tap is fully open and hoses are not kinked.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet filters
fixSteps.instruction: Clean mesh filters at both ends of the inlet hose.

fixSteps.title: Test pre-wash valve
fixSteps.instruction: On dual-valve models, test the pre-wash valve solenoid specifically.

fixSteps.title: Replace valve
fixSteps.instruction: If the pre-wash valve has failed, replace the inlet valve assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the valve needs replacement on a dual-valve model.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E11 pre-wash fill error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E10 and E11?
faq.a: E10 is fill timeout during main wash, E11 is during pre-wash. Same causes typically apply.

faq.q: Do all models have pre-wash?
faq.a: Most do. Some budget models skip pre-wash and would not show E11.

faq.q: Can I skip pre-wash to avoid E11?
faq.a: Yes, select a program without pre-wash. But the underlying fill issue should still be investigated.

title: Water Leak Detected
shortDescription: The anti-flood sensor has detected water in the base tray.
fullDescription: Error E13 indicates water has leaked into the base tray of the washing machine. The float switch activates the drain pump and blocks further water intake to prevent flooding.

fixSteps.title: Tilt and drain
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug, turn off water. Tilt machine to drain base tray.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door seal
fixSteps.instruction: Check for tears, gaps, or detergent buildup preventing proper seal.

fixSteps.title: Check hoses and clamps
fixSteps.instruction: Remove panels and inspect all internal hose connections.

fixSteps.title: Run test and identify leak
fixSteps.instruction: Run a short cycle while watching for the leak source.

whenToCallPro: If the leak is from the tub seal or drum bearings.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E13 water leak anti flood fix

faq.q: Can too much detergent cause E13?
faq.a: Yes, excessive suds can overflow from the detergent drawer into the base.

faq.q: Is E13 always a serious leak?
faq.a: Not always. Sometimes it is just detergent overflow or a minor hose clamp issue.

faq.q: How does the anti-flood work?
faq.a: A polystyrene float in the base tray rises with water, triggering a microswitch.

title: Drain Pump Timeout
shortDescription: The washer could not drain the water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error E20 indicates the drain cycle did not complete within the expected time. The drain pump filter may be blocked, the hose obstructed, or the pump has failed.
symptoms: No pump sound or strained pump

fixSteps.title: Clean drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open access panel at bottom-front. Place towels below. Unscrew filter cap and remove debris.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect for kinks and blockages. Disconnect from standpipe and check.

fixSteps.title: Inspect pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With filter removed, check impeller spins freely.

fixSteps.title: Replace drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If motor dead, disconnect and replace.

videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E20 drain pump blocked fix

faq.q: How do I manually drain?
faq.a: Open the filter panel, place container below, slowly unscrew the filter cap.

faq.q: How often should I clean the filter?
faq.a: Every 1-2 months.

faq.q: Can the standpipe cause E20?
faq.a: Yes, a blocked wall drain prevents the machine from emptying.

title: Drain Pump Not Responding
shortDescription: The drain pump is not receiving power or the pump motor has failed electrically.
fullDescription: Error E21 indicates the drain pump circuit has an electrical fault. Unlike E20 (blocked drain), E21 means the pump itself is not running — either no power reaches it or the motor winding has failed.

fixSteps.title: Check pump connector
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the electrical connector to the drain pump is firmly plugged in.

fixSteps.title: Test pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect pump and measure resistance (5-30 ohms).

fixSteps.title: Check for voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Reconnect and measure for mains voltage during drain cycle.

fixSteps.title: Replace pump
fixSteps.instruction: If motor is open circuit, replace it.

whenToCallPro: If pump is good but no voltage reaches it — board relay failure.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E21 drain pump no power fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E20 and E21?
faq.a: E20 = pump runs but blockage prevents draining. E21 = pump does not run at all (electrical fault).

faq.q: Can I manually drain?
faq.a: Yes, use the filter access at the bottom-front.

faq.q: How long do pumps last?
faq.a: Typically 8-12 years.

title: Door Lock Fault
shortDescription: The door interlock is not engaging or signaling correctly.
fullDescription: Error E40 indicates the door lock mechanism has not engaged or confirmed it is locked. The machine cannot start a cycle without confirming the door is securely locked. This is one of the most common Zanussi error codes.
symptoms: Door lock clicks but does not hold

fixSteps.title: Check door closure
fixSteps.instruction: Push door firmly. Check nothing prevents full closure.

fixSteps.title: Inspect catch and striker
fixSteps.instruction: Examine for wear or damage. Replace if needed.

fixSteps.title: Test interlock
fixSteps.instruction: Access from inside door rim. Test continuity across terminals.

fixSteps.title: Replace interlock
fixSteps.instruction: Peel back door seal, remove retaining band, swap interlock.

whenToCallPro: If interlock and catch test fine, the board may have a faulty output.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E40 door lock fix

faq.q: How do I open a stuck door?
faq.a: Wait 2-3 minutes after turning off. Use the emergency release behind the filter panel.

faq.q: Is E40 the same as E41?
faq.a: E40 is a general door lock fault. E41 and E42 are more specific sub-codes on some models.

faq.q: Can I bypass the door lock?
faq.a: Never. It prevents the door opening during high-speed spin.

title: Door Not Closed
shortDescription: The control board detects the door is not physically closed.
fullDescription: Error E41 specifically indicates the door closure switch does not detect the door is in the closed position. This is different from E40 (lock mechanism fault) — E41 means the door is physically not shutting.
symptoms: Gap visible between door and seal

fixSteps.title: Clear obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Check for clothing, debris, or buildup in the door seal preventing closure.

fixSteps.title: Check door hinge
fixSteps.instruction: Open and close the door slowly. If it sags or does not align with the catch, the hinge may need adjustment or replacement.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door catch
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the catch engages the striker plate firmly.

fixSteps.title: Replace catch or hinge
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the worn or broken component.

whenToCallPro: If the hinge needs professional alignment or the door frame is bent.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E41 door not closing fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E40 and E41?
faq.a: E40 = lock mechanism fault. E41 = door physically not closed.

faq.q: Can a sagging hinge cause E41?
faq.a: Yes, a worn hinge prevents proper door alignment with the catch.

faq.q: Is E41 common?
faq.a: Yes, especially on older machines where the door hinge has worn.

title: Door Lock Microswitch Fault
shortDescription: The door lock microswitch is not confirming the locked state after the interlock engaged.
fullDescription: Error E42 specifically indicates the door lock mechanism engaged (solenoid activated) but the microswitch that confirms the locked state did not change. The machine cannot start without full lock confirmation.

causes.cause: Door interlock microswitch worn or failed

symptoms: Door lock engages but machine does not start
Machine tries to start then stops

fixSteps.title: Reset and retry
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes. Open and close door firmly. Try again.

fixSteps.title: Test interlock microswitch
fixSteps.instruction: Access the interlock. With the door closed, test continuity across the confirmation microswitch pins.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect wiring from the interlock to the control board for damage or loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Replace the interlock
fixSteps.instruction: If the microswitch is faulty, replace the entire interlock assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the interlock tests fine, the board input circuit may be faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E42 door lock microswitch fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E40, E41, and E42?
faq.a: E40 = general lock fault, E41 = door not closed, E42 = locked but microswitch not confirming.

faq.q: Can I hear the lock engage with E42?
faq.a: Usually yes — you hear the click but the machine still will not start because the confirmation switch failed.

faq.q: Is the microswitch separate from the interlock?
faq.a: No, it is built into the interlock assembly, so the whole unit is replaced.

title: Motor Speed Sensor / Tacho Fault
shortDescription: The motor speed sensor is not providing a valid signal.
fullDescription: Error E50 indicates the motor tachometer or speed sensor is not sending expected feedback to the control board. The board cannot regulate drum speed safely without this signal. Worn carbon brushes are the most common cause.
symptoms: Motor hums but does not turn

fixSteps.title: Check carbon brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Remove rear panel, extract brushes from motor. Replace if shorter than 1 cm.

fixSteps.title: Test tachometer
fixSteps.instruction: Measure tacho coil resistance (100-200 ohms). Check magnet is secure.

fixSteps.title: Test motor windings
fixSteps.instruction: Measure winding resistance (2-8 ohms). Check for ground faults.

fixSteps.title: Replace worn parts
fixSteps.instruction: Replace brushes, tacho, or motor as needed.

whenToCallPro: If brushes and tacho are fine, the motor or control board may need replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E50 motor carbon brushes fix

faq.q: How long do carbon brushes last?
faq.a: Typically 5-8 years.

faq.q: Can I replace brushes myself?
faq.a: Yes, it is one of the most common DIY washer repairs.

faq.q: What are carbon brushes?
faq.a: Small carbon blocks that make electrical contact with the spinning motor commutator.

title: Motor Triac Short Circuit
shortDescription: The motor triac on the control board has short-circuited.
fullDescription: Error E51 indicates the triac controlling the motor has failed. The motor may run uncontrolled or not at all. This is a control board component failure.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test motor
fixSteps.instruction: Check motor windings for shorts and ground faults.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check motor wiring for damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board
fixSteps.instruction: If motor is fine, replace the board.

whenToCallPro: E51 requires board replacement. Professional recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E51 motor triac error fix

faq.q: Can the triac be replaced alone?
faq.a: With electronics soldering, but full board replacement is standard.

faq.q: Will a surge protector help?
faq.a: Yes, it protects against voltage spikes.

faq.q: Is E51 dangerous?
faq.a: If the motor runs uncontrolled, unplug immediately.

title: Motor Tacho Signal Lost
shortDescription: The tacho signal was present but has been lost during operation.
fullDescription: Error E52 specifically means the tachometer signal was detected at some point but then lost during the cycle. This differs from E50 where no signal is detected at all. E52 often indicates an intermittent connection.

causes.cause: Tacho magnet coming loose during spin

symptoms: Error only at high spin speeds

fixSteps.title: Reduce load size
fixSteps.instruction: Overloading can cause intermittent motor issues. Try a smaller load.

fixSteps.title: Check tacho connector
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the tacho wiring connector is firmly seated on the motor.

fixSteps.title: Inspect carbon brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Brushes at borderline length can cause intermittent contact. Replace if under 1.5 cm.

fixSteps.title: Check tacho magnet
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the magnet on the motor shaft is secure and not spinning free.

whenToCallPro: If the issue persists after checking brushes and tacho.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E52 intermittent tacho error fix

faq.q: Why does E52 only happen at high spin?
faq.a: Intermittent connections or borderline brushes may work at low speed but fail under the higher current demands of fast spin.

faq.q: Is E52 the same as E50?
faq.a: E50 means no signal at all. E52 means the signal was there but was lost, suggesting an intermittent fault.

faq.q: Can overloading cause E52?
faq.a: Yes, an overloaded drum puts extra strain on the motor, potentially causing brush contact issues.

title: Motor Relay Contact Fault
shortDescription: The motor relay on the control board is stuck or the motor circuit has a fault.
fullDescription: Error E54 indicates the motor relay contacts on the main control board have failed — either stuck closed (motor powered continuously) or stuck open (motor not powered). This is a control board level fault affecting the motor power circuit.
symptoms: Motor runs when it should not
Motor will not start at all

fixSteps.title: Safety power off
fixSteps.instruction: If motor is running uncontrolled, unplug immediately.

fixSteps.title: Test motor
fixSteps.instruction: Check motor windings and insulation for faults that could damage the relay.

fixSteps.title: Inspect relay on board
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burnt or discolored relay on the control board.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the main control board. Also replace motor if it caused the relay failure.

whenToCallPro: E54 is a board-level fault. Professional repair recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E54 motor relay board fix

faq.q: Is E54 dangerous?
faq.a: If the motor runs uncontrolled, yes. Unplug immediately.

faq.q: Can the relay be replaced alone?
faq.a: With skilled soldering, but board replacement is standard.

faq.q: What causes relay failure?
faq.a: Motor short circuits, aging, and power surges.

title: Heating Element Fault
shortDescription: The wash heating element is not working correctly.
fullDescription: Error E60 indicates the heating element circuit has a fault. The element may be open circuit, shorted to ground, or the control board heater circuit has failed.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test heater
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance (20-30 ohms) and insulation (over 2 megaohms).

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect heater terminals for corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Replace heater
fixSteps.instruction: Remove old element, install replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the heater trips the RCD or the board relay is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E60 heater element fix

faq.q: Can I wash with cold water?
faq.a: Possible but cleaning will be poor.

faq.q: Why does the RCD trip?
faq.a: A heater leaking to ground trips the safety switch.

faq.q: How long does a heater last?
faq.a: Typically 7-12 years.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The water temperature sensor is out of range or open circuit.
fullDescription: Error E61 indicates the NTC thermistor is reading outside expected range. Without valid temperature data, the washer cannot heat water safely.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test NTC
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect NTC from heater housing. Measure resistance (~20K ohms at 20°C).

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect NTC wiring from sensor to board.

fixSteps.title: Replace NTC
fixSteps.instruction: Pull old sensor out, insert new one.

whenToCallPro: Only if NTC and wiring test fine.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E61 NTC temperature sensor fix

faq.q: What is an NTC sensor?
faq.a: A thermistor whose resistance decreases as temperature increases.

faq.q: Is the NTC expensive?
faq.a: No, typically 5-15 for the part.

faq.q: Can I still wash?
faq.a: The machine may run but will not heat water.

title: Water Overheating
shortDescription: The water temperature has exceeded the safe maximum.
fullDescription: Error E62 indicates the NTC sensor detected the water temperature exceeding the safe maximum (typically above 88°C). The machine stops immediately to prevent damage. This can indicate a stuck heater relay or faulty NTC.

fixSteps.title: Unplug immediately
fixSteps.instruction: If overheating, unplug right away for safety.

fixSteps.title: Test NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Check if NTC is reading correctly. A faulty NTC may trigger a false overheat.

fixSteps.title: Test heater relay
fixSteps.instruction: With power off, check if heater has continuity through the relay even when it should be off. If yes, relay is welded.

fixSteps.title: Replace board
fixSteps.instruction: If relay is stuck, replace the control board.

whenToCallPro: E62 is safety-critical. Professional repair recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E62 overheating relay fix

faq.q: Is E62 dangerous?
faq.a: Yes, overheating is a safety hazard. Unplug immediately.

faq.q: Can a faulty NTC cause E62?
faq.a: Yes, an NTC reading too low makes the board think the water is cold and keeps heating.

faq.q: What temperature triggers E62?
faq.a: Typically above 88°C.

title: Communication / PCB Error
shortDescription: Communication error between electronic modules or general PCB fault.
fullDescription: Error E90 indicates a communication failure between the main control board and the display or user interface module. This is often caused by a loose cable connection or failed board.

fixSteps.title: Reseat connections
fixSteps.instruction: Open top panel, reseat cable between display and main board.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect boards and connectors for corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes after reseating.

fixSteps.title: Replace display board
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating fails, replace the display board first.

whenToCallPro: If unsure which board is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E90 communication error fix

faq.q: Can steam cause E90?
faq.a: Yes, moisture corrodes board connectors.

faq.q: Which board first?
faq.a: Start with cable, then display, then main board.

faq.q: Is E90 common?
faq.a: Moderately common, especially in humid environments.

title: Control Board Communication Timeout
shortDescription: The main control board has lost communication with the user interface for too long.
fullDescription: Error E91 is a timeout variant of E90 — the control board lost communication with the display/UI module and could not re-establish it within the allowed time. This is usually a more persistent fault than E90.

causes.cause: Cable between boards damaged or broken

symptoms: Machine appears off despite being plugged in

fixSteps.title: Full power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 minutes for complete discharge.

fixSteps.title: Check and replace cable
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the interconnect cable. Replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Test with new display
fixSteps.instruction: If cable is fine, replace the display board.

fixSteps.title: Replace main board
fixSteps.instruction: If display replacement does not fix it, the main board needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If a new display board does not resolve E91.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer E91 board communication timeout fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E90 and E91?
faq.a: E90 is a general communication error. E91 is a timeout — communication was lost and did not recover.

faq.q: Is E91 more serious than E90?
faq.a: Often yes, as it indicates a persistent rather than intermittent fault.

faq.q: Can a power problem cause E91?
faq.a: Unstable power can cause communication failures. Try a different outlet.

title: Excessive Foam Detected
shortDescription: The machine has detected excessive foam/suds during the wash cycle.
fullDescription: Error EF0 indicates the control board has detected excessive suds in the drum. Too much foam interferes with the wash action, drain pump operation, and can cause leaks. The machine may attempt extra rinse cycles to clear the foam.

causes.cause: Pressure switch misreading due to foam in the hose
Drain system partially blocked trapping foam

symptoms: Machine takes much longer than expected

fixSteps.title: Reduce detergent
fixSteps.instruction: For the next wash, use significantly less detergent. Modern machines and detergents are concentrated — use the recommended amount on the detergent packaging.

fixSteps.title: Run a rinse cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Run an empty rinse and spin cycle to clear remaining foam from the system.

fixSteps.title: Check detergent type
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure you are using a low-suds or HE (high efficiency) detergent suitable for front-loading machines. Hand wash or top-loader detergents produce too much foam.

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Foam residue can accumulate in the drain filter. Clean it thoroughly.

whenToCallPro: EF0 is almost always a user error with detergent. No professional needed unless the error persists with correct detergent amounts.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi washer EF0 too much foam suds fix

faq.q: How much detergent should I use?
faq.a: Follow the instructions on the detergent packaging. For concentrated detergents, typically one small capful or one tablet is sufficient.

faq.q: Can EF0 damage the machine?
faq.a: Excessive foam can leak through seals and damage the control board or trigger the anti-flood system. Always use the correct detergent amount.

faq.q: What is HE detergent?
faq.a: High Efficiency detergent is formulated to produce less suds while still cleaning effectively. It is essential for front-loading machines.
